entry.title: TeamPCP Breaches Cloud, SaaS Instances With Stolen Credentials
entry.content:
The threat group's shift to speedy attacks on AWS, Azure, and SaaS instances shows organizations need to respond quickly to compromised credentials.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Axios NPM Package Compromised in Precision Attack
entry.content:
The NPM package for Axios, a popular JavaScript HTTP client library, was briefly compromised this week, possibly by North Korean threat actors.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: The Forgotten Endpoint: Security Risks of Dormant Devices
entry.content:
Technology Talk: That forgotten notebook holds plenty of secrets to enterprise access.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Venom Stealer MaaS Platform Commoditizes ClickFix Attacks
entry.content:
A new service on the cybercrime market provides automated capabilities to create persistent information-stealing social engineering attacks.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Cyberattacks Intensify Pressure on Latin American Governments
entry.content:
Cyber threats across Latin America are increasingly targeting government systems, from disruptive attacks in Puerto Rico to a surge of probes against Colombia’s health sector.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: LatAm's Self-Taught Cyber Talent Overlooked Amid Cyberattack Glut
entry.content:
A newly released study exclusively shared with Dark Reading details the unique circumstances that make up Latin America's labor pool, and why organizations may want to expand their talent search.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Ransomware Will Hit Hospitals. Rehearsals Are Key to Defense
entry.content:
A chief medical information officer describes what hospitals face when they inevitably suffer a ransomware attack—whether it leads to short- or long-term outages.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: RSAC 2026: AI Dominates, But Community Remains Key to Security
entry.content:
As AI took center stage at this year's conference, experts debated automation, oversight and the evolving role of human intelligence in cybersecurity — despite the US government's notable absence.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Security Bosses Are All-In on AI. Here's Why
entry.content:
CISOs are bullish on AI and have big plans to roll out future tools. We talk to Reddit CISO Frederick Lee and leading analyst Dave Gruber about how AI is working out in the real world, as well as its future promise.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Not Toying Around: Hasbro Attack May Take 'Weeks' to Remediate
entry.content:
The company's 8-K filing notes "unauthorized access" and that it's activated business continuity plans and taken some systems offline.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Geopolitics, AI, and Cybersecurity: Insights From RSAC 2026
entry.content:
AI-driven threats, global leadership shifts, and the future of cybersecurity in a rapidly evolving landscape were among the discussions at RSAC 2026 Conference.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Linx Security Raises $50 Million for Identity Security and Governance
entry.content:
The company will accelerate product development, scale go-to-market efforts, and expand its global footprint. The post Linx Security Raises $50 Million for Identity Security and Governance appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Mercor Hit by LiteLLM Supply Chain Attack
entry.content:
The AI recruiting firm is investigating the incident as Lapsus$ claimed the theft of 4TB of Mercor data. The post Mercor Hit by LiteLLM Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: 250,000 Affected by Data Breach at Nacogdoches Memorial Hospital
entry.content:
In January 2026, a threat actor hacked the hospital’s internal network and stole personal and health information. The post 250,000 Affected by Data Breach at Nacogdoches Memorial Hospital appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Cybersecurity M&A Roundup: 38 Deals Announced in March 2026
entry.content:
Significant cybersecurity M&A deals announced by Airbus, Cellebrite, Databricks, Quantum eMotion, Rapid7, and OpenAI. The post Cybersecurity M&A Roundup: 38 Deals Announced in March 2026 appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Apple Rolls Out DarkSword Exploit Protection to More Devices
entry.content:
The DarkSword exploit kit has been used by both state-sponsored hackers and commercial spyware vendors. The post Apple Rolls Out DarkSword Exploit Protection to More Devices appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Critical Vulnerability in Claude Code Emerges Days After Source Leak
entry.content:
Within days of each other, Anthropic first leaked the source code to Claude Code, and then a critical vulnerability was found by Adversa AI. The post Critical Vulnerability in Claude Code Emerges Days After Source Leak appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Nissan says stolen data came from third-party vendor after hacking group claims breach
entry.content:
A hacking group claimed this week to have breached the file-transfer system used by a company that offers services to Nissan and Infiniti dealerships across North America. Nissan said there was no indication "customer information was accessed or put at risk."
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Crypto platform Drift suspends services after millions stolen in security incident
entry.content:
Security experts believe hundreds of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency were stolen from decentralized finance platform Drift on Wednesday after the site confirmed it was experiencing a cyberattack.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Drift crypto platform confirms $280 million stolen in hack as researchers point finger at North Korea
entry.content:
The platform released a post-mortem on Wednesday night explaining that malicious actors gained access to Drift systems through a “novel attack” that involved the “rapid takeover” of the company’s security council administrative powers.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
entry.content:
Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-as-a-service offering lets customers sidestep both of these pitfalls: It uses cleverly disguised links to load the target brand's real website, and then acts as a relay between the target and the legitimate site -- forwarding the victim's username, password and multi-factor authentic
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
entry.content:
AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and n
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
entry.content:
A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
entry.content:
The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
entry.content:
A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: National Aerospace Fasteners
entry.content:

🎭 Threat Actor: WORLDLEAKS | 📰 Source: Red Packet Security | 🏷️ Type: Ransomware

entry.title: Meet Claude Mythos: Leaked Anthropic post reveals the powerful upcoming model
entry.content:
Matt Binder reports: An accidental leak has now been officially confirmed by AI company Anthropic regarding its most powerful AI model yet. The model, now known as “Claude Mythos,” was originally uncovered in a report from Fortune. Anthropic has since confirmed the details about the leak to the outlet. The data leak included details about the upcoming release of the... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Thousands of Corewell Health patients affected by security breach
entry.content:
Fox2 reports: Corewell Health announced their former health care consulting vendor experienced a data breach in 2024, affecting thousands of patients. Pinnacle Holdings previously provided health care consulting services to Corewell Health. After being notified of the data breach, Corewell said they conducted a review to identify who was impacted. The health system said around... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Woodfords Family Services notifying patients and families about 2024 ransomware attack
entry.content:
A notice by Woodfords Family Services in Maine caught my eye because the name sounded familiar. They provide support services for people with disabilities and their families. On March 27, 2026, they issued a notice: What Happened? On April 8, 2024, we discovered suspicious activity within our network. We took steps to secure our environment and... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Iran-linked group Handala hacked FBI Director Kash Patel’s personal email account
entry.content:
Pierluigi Paganini reports: Iran-linked hacking group Handala claims it breached FBI Director Kash Patel’s personal Gmail account and shared alleged data, including photos and files. The FBI confirmed it is aware of the incident and has taken steps to mitigate risks, stressing that the exposed material is old and does not involve any government or classified information.... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Thankfully, the Infinite Campus incident did not involve a lot of non-directory student information
entry.content:
In the wake of the Infinite Campus data breach, DataBreaches was contacted by several concerned EdTech professionals who weren’t prepared to accept Infinite Campus’s word that there was no sensitive student information in the data tranche. With their encouragement, DataBreaches downloaded the data tranche from ShinyHunters’ leak site and examined it. Most of the files... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: CareCloud notifies the SEC after attack on one of its EHR environments
entry.content:
DysruptionHub reports: CareCloud said an unauthorized third party briefly disrupted one of its six electronic health record environments on March 16, restoring access that evening as investigators assess possible patient data exposure. In a March 27 SEC filing, the Somerset, New Jersey-based company said an unauthorized third party temporarily accessed part of its CareCloud Health division... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Did you sign up for the new White House app? Don’t use it until you read this!
entry.content:
Did you sign up for the new White House app? Don’t use it until you read this, because it puts your privacy and data security at risk. Patrick Quirk takes an impressive technical piece and distills it for those of us who are not developers or coders. His article is based on original research by... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: South African government agency and Spanish psychological software provider victims of cyberattacks by XP95
entry.content:
Jan Vermeulen reports: Statistics South Africa has become the latest government entity to fall victim to a ransomware attack by the emerging cybercrime group known as XP95. The threat actors claim to have successfully breached the agency responsible for conducting South Africa’s census, as well as producing and disseminating other official statistics, like the Consumer... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Estonian hospital sends patient home with other peoples’ health data
entry.content:
Märten Hallismaa reports: A person who underwent X-ray imaging at West Tallinn Central Hospital (LTKH) was sent home with a USB drive that also contained the personal and health data of other patients. ERR has received information about a person who went to West Tallinn Central Hospital to undergo an X-ray. Because the images needed... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Attack on axios software developer tool threatens widespread compromises
entry.content:
Tim Starks and Derek B. Johnson report: A hacker briefly delivered malware this week through a popular open-source project for software developers that has an estimated 100 million weekly downloads, raising the possibility of compromises spreading widely through a supply-chain attack. Axios is a JavaScript client library used in web requests. The unknown attacker hijacked... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: New EvilTokens service fuels Microsoft device code phishing attacks
entry.content:
A new malicious kit called EvilTokens integrates device code phishing capabilities, allowing attackers to hijack Microsoft accounts and provide advanced features for business email compromise attacks. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Hackers exploit TrueConf zero-day to push malicious software updates
entry.content:
Hackers have targeted TrueConf conference servers in attacks that exploit a zero-day vulnerability, allowing them to execute arbitrary files on all connected endpoints. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Apple expands iOS 18 updates to more iPhones to block DarkSword attacks
entry.content:
Apple has now made it possible for more iPhones still running iOS 18 to receive security updates that protect against the actively exploited DarkSword exploit kit. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Over 14,000 F5 BIG-IP APM instances still exposed to RCE attacks
entry.content:
Internet security watchdog Shadowserver has found over 14,000 BIG-IP APM instances exposed online amid ongoing attacks exploiting a critical-severity remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Critical Cisco IMC auth bypass gives attackers Admin access
entry.content:
Cisco has patched several critical and high-severity vulnerabilities, including an Integrated Management Controller (IMC) authentication bypass that enables attackers to gain Admin access. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Medtech giant Stryker fully operational after data-wiping attack
entry.content:
Stryker Corporation, one of the world's leading medical technology companies, says it's fully operational three weeks after many of its systems were wiped out in a cyberattack claimed by the Iranian-linked Handala hacktivist group. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: New Progress ShareFile flaws can be chained in pre-auth RCE attacks
entry.content:
Two vulnerabilities in Progress ShareFile, an enterprise-grade secure file transfer solution, can be chained to enable unauthenticated file exfiltration from affected environments. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Drift loses $280 million North Korean hackers seize Security Council powers
entry.content:
The Drift Protocol lost at least $280 million after a threat actor took control of its Security Council administrative powers in a planned, sophisticated operation. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Claude Code leak used to push infostealer malware on GitHub
entry.content:
Threat actors are exploiting the recent Claude Code source code leak by using fake GitHub repositories to deliver Vidar information-stealing malware. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: CERT-EU: European Commission hack exposes data of 30 EU entities
entry.content:
The European Union's Cybersecurity Service (CERT-EU) has attributed the European Commission cloud hack to the TeamPCP threat group, saying the resulting breach exposed the data of at least 29 other Union entities. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Data Breaches Announced by Corewell Health & Rocky Mountain Care
entry.content:
Rocky Mountain Care in Utah has announced a January 2026 data breach, and Corewell Health in Michigan has confirmed that […] The post Data Breaches Announced by Corewell Health & Rocky Mountain Care appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: FREE Webinar: Workforce HIPAA Compliance – Building Your First Line of Defense
entry.content:
Many organizations invest heavily in technology and policies, but overlook the single biggest driver of compliance risk: their workforce. This […] The post FREE Webinar: Workforce HIPAA Compliance – Building Your First Line of Defense appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Data Breach Reported by Orthopedic Implant Manufacturer TriMed
entry.content:
TriMed, a Santa Clarita, California-based manufacturer of upper and lower orthopedic implants, has announced a data security incident involving unauthorized […] The post Data Breach Reported by Orthopedic Implant Manufacturer TriMed appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Free HIPAA Compliance Risk Calculator
entry.content:
HIPAA compliance is mandatory for organizations that qualify as HIPAA covered entities. But how compliant is your organization really? With our 5-minute free HIPAA Risk Check, you can quickly evaluate the compliance status of your organization and receive a report with actionable insights to immediately improve compliance with HIPAA. The post Free HIPAA Compliance Risk Calculator appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Nacogdoches Memorial Hospital Data Breach More Than 257,000 Individuals
entry.content:
Nacogdoches Memorial Hospital (NMH), a 226-bed hospital in Nacogdoches, Texas, has recently announced a data security incident that was first […] The post Nacogdoches Memorial Hospital Data Breach More Than 257,000 Individuals appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Eye Physicians of Central Florida Data Breach Settlement
entry.content:
Eye Physicians of Central Florida has agreed to settle a class action lawsuit stemming from a 2023 data breach that […] The post Eye Physicians of Central Florida Data Breach Settlement appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Iowa AG Sues Change Healthcare Over 2024 Ransomware Attack
entry.content:
Iowa Attorney General Brenna Bird has filed a lawsuit against Change Healthcare, UnitedHealth Group, and Optum over the February 2024 […] The post Iowa AG Sues Change Healthcare Over 2024 Ransomware Attack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Cardiovascular Consultants Pays $3.85M to Settle Data Breach Litigation
entry.content:
Cardiovascular Consultants in Arizona has settled a class action lawsuit stemming from a 2023 data breach involving the protected health […] The post Cardiovascular Consultants Pays $3.85M to Settle Data Breach Litigation appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.content:
Unknown reported a healthcare breach.
